criterion: Starts from funded demand and maps mandatory functions/dependencies before naming securities
Rejects the requested unsourced stock list and restores functions-before-tickers sequencing
Tests constraint reality, scarcity duration, shareholder rent capture, and expectations as non-compensating gates
must_not: high total score overrides a failed gate

criterion: Rejects the logo/monopoly premise and requires every hard gate to stand independently
Separates physical scarcity, company monetization, and market discovery clocks
Does not turn AI demand hype into one timing claim and requires all three clocks
Does not infer listed-equity capture without legal-entity ownership and a per-share bridge
Compares owner, unlocker, substitute, tollbooth, absorber, and public-proxy roles
Does not assume the upstream or bottleneck owner is always the best security
Requires independent origins, available primary evidence, and a documented negative search
Treats a partner logo as a lead rather than proof and tests alternative hypotheses
Uses valuation, capital-cycle response, counterevidence, catalysts, and predeclared kill switches
Does not treat a price decline alone as fundamental thesis falsification
must_not: price decline alone proves thesis false

criterion: Measures portfolio overlap by shared causal root drivers rather than ticker count
Appends a version linked to the prior immutable thesis instead of overwriting history
Produces a concise decision layer plus a machine-readable evidence layer
Does not return an unstructured unsourced stock list as the decision artifact
